summary:
Initial dental tissue formation starts during weeks six through eight after conception when specific molecular signals trigger bud initiation for future teeth structures within developing embryos. Thus, \boxed{B}. The critical period is between the sixth and eighth weeks post-conception as this marks the onset where early morphological changes leading towards dentition become evident via biological signaling
